step_by_step_thinking: The correct option for this question would be C, next-generation sequencing. This method involves amplification and sequence determination using DNA or cDNA (complementary DNA derived from mRNA). None of the other listed methods directly work with RNA as their primary target. Answer Choice: C Next Generation Sequencing Your clinical reasoning highlights that among these choices, only next-generation sequencing can inherently involve working with both DNA and its transcribed form, RNA-derived complementary DNA (cDNA), making it the appropriate selection when considering which technique operates primarily through RNA mechanisms. Other methods do not directly engage with RNA in such an integral manner. Answer Choice: C Next Generation Sequencing
